quotes: Do the boring things (first): eat healthy, exercise, work hard, say I love you and spend time with your family... nothing else matters.
Risk comes with freedom. Security comes with limitations. Mindfulness is weighing how much freedom one wants balancing how much risk one can manage.
Life is full of challenges. Either you pick them or they're picked for you. The most important thing is to enjoy the ride.

Working out will make you feel weak, but it actually makes you stronger. Learning will make you feel dumb, but it actually makes you smarter. Investing (in yourself) will make you feel broke, but it actually makes you richer. Facing your fears will make you feel terrified, but it actually makes you braver. Do the hard thing ... It makes you better. Trust the process
Speaking the truth comes at a price, but revealing a falsehood carries an even higher cost.

If you're having a bad day think of three different ways your day could have been worse. If you find them, feel better ... It could have been worse. If you don't, you haven't thought thoroughly enough

If your source of information is one-sided, there's no room for you to gain knowledge. If your source of ideas is single-minded, there's no room for you to grow. If your source of news is based on one story, there's no room for you to view the big picture. Make sure you listen to the other side!
Happiness is doing what you like to do with the people you like.
You have never truly lived till the moment you realize that you'd happily give your life for someone else
Risk comes with freedom. Security comes with limitations. Mindfulness is weighing how much freedom one wants balancing how much risk one can manage.
Life is full of challenges. Either you pick them or they're picked for you. The most important thing is to enjoy the ride, as at the end you're not taking anything (good or bad) with you.
The opposite of perfect is doing nothing. The opposite of perfect is NOT doing something imperfect or imperfectly. 20% of doing it perfectly is infinitely more than 100% of doing nothing

You are eating food to enjoy (emotional reason) and to stay/become healthy (pragmatic reason) In a world with more food than we need, make sure you are consuming food for at most both reasons. Otherwise, fasting is very helpful.
in the light of playing an infinite game: What are you (doing) that leads to the success being a side effect?

Having a great IQ matters ... Being a smart person matters more. Having a pretty face matters ... Being a beautiful person matters more. Having friendships matters ... Being a good friend matters more. If you want to achieve something, become the person you need to be first ... The rest will take care of itself.
We are all juggling the 6 Fs: Faith (Integrity), Fitness (mental and physical health), Family, Finance, Fun and Friends. Which one(s) of these balls you'd drop to keep juggling the others?
When you lie to somebody you're lying to yourself first thinking it's ok.
Hammer, avel or something in-between: What you'd rather be?
What's your purpose? Why are you here?
What's wrong with you? And do you need help to make it right?
What are you selling? And what are you trading your goods for?
What's THE thing you're good at? Are you known being good at that thing?
What's your biggest mistake? Was it the best lesson you learned?
What would you rather be: feared or respected? And what does this say about you?
What makes you worthy of love? What doesn't make you worthy of love?
Whom are you really angry with? What happens if you forgive and/or forget?
What do you spend the majority of your awake time on? Is it worth it?
How do you see yourself in 5 years? And what are you doing now to achieve this vision?
What is the thing you can do today, that can make you better than you were yesterday?
I'm less interested in people with the correct answers and more in poeple with challenging questions.
You'd never stop being a child untill you find something you live for. And you'd never completely mature untill you find something you'd die for.
You play a game you didn't choose with cards that are not fairly dealt in a time frame you know nothing about. The question is: do you want to spend your time whining about how
you are? Or invest your time increasing the value of your cards to make the best out of them?
The pain will leave you as soon as it's done teaching you the lessons - Bruce Lee
Your words are a currency. It loses value every time they don't match your actions.
The only way to know where the line is, is to cross it. And the only thing keeping you from crossing it is you!
The real success is ending up with lessons learned. The real failure is not working hard enough.
If you don't doubt you'd never know for sure.
Even the colorblind doesn't only see black and white. Why would you want to!?
No risk is a missed opportunity; too much risk is a broken timeline.
